channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Uganda confirms three additional Ebola cases as total rises to five amid regional outbreak concerns
item.description: Uganda health authorities don confirm say three new Ebola cases don appear for di country, bringing di total number of confirmed infections to five since di outbreak start on May 15, 2026. Di announce...

item.title: Ruto Announces KSh 5 Million Scholarship Programme for 250 Students at Lunga Lunga TVC
item.description: President William Ruto has announced a KSh 5 million scholarship programme aimed at supporting 250 students from Lunga Lunga Constituency to pursue short technical courses at the Lunga Lunga Technical...

item.title: Ontario Review Board allows supervised community placement for Toronto man found not criminally responsible in 2013 firefighter killing
item.description: Nabil Huruy, a 35-year-old Toronto man found not criminally responsible for the 2013 killing of off-duty firefighter Dominic Parker because of a mental disorder, has transitioned from a secure forensi...

item.title: Authors at Hay Festival recommend books to rekindle a love of reading
item.description: A wide range of writers, thinkers and public figures appearing at the Hay Festival have shared personal recommendations of books they believe can help readers rediscover a love of reading. The selecti...

item.title: Senegal president dismisses prime minister and dissolves government amid political and economic tensions
item.description: Senegal’s President Bassirou Diomaye Faye has dismissed Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko and dissolved the entire government following months of growing political friction between the two former allies. T...

item.title: AFP chief pays tribute to airmen killed in Benguet training flight crash
item.description: The Chief of Staff of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP), General Romeo S Brawner Jr., has paid tribute to two Philippine Air Force (PAF) airmen who died in a training flight crash in Tuba, Ben...

item.title: Pendlebury credits Collingwood crowd after hard-fought win over West Coast in milestone game
item.description: Collingwood veteran Scott Pendlebury has praised the club’s supporters after a spirited and physically demanding win over West Coast, who competed under the name Waalitj Marawar for Sir Doug Nicholls ...

item.title: Amanda Platell reflects on Kylie Minogue’s documentary and shared experiences of infertility and IVF
item.description: In this opinion column, Amanda Platell reflects on watching Kylie Minogue’s documentary and how it unexpectedly moved her emotionally. Although she admits she has never been particularly interested in...

item.title: Londie London launches solo reality series focusing on personal life, motherhood and career
item.description: South African reality television personality and musician Londie London, whose real name is Londiwe Siphiwokuhle Zulu, has launched a new solo reality series titled Life With Londie, airing on Mzansi ...

item.title: Albanese defends housing tax reforms amid debate over negative gearing and capital gains changes
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has defended his government’s changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax settings, insisting the reforms are necessary to improve housing affordability for young...

item.title: Paul McCartney Guests on Final ‘Late Show’ Episode, Performing ‘Hello Goodbye’ With Stephen Colbert at Ed Sullivan Theater Farewell
item.description: The final episode of ‘The Late Show With Stephen Colbert’ featured a surprise and symbolic appearance by Paul McCartney, who helped close out the long-running program with a memorable musical and emot...

item.title: US imposes visa ban on Tanzanian senior police officer over alleged human rights abuses
item.description: The United States has imposed sanctions on a senior officer of the Tanzania Police Force, Senior Assistant Commissioner Faustine Jackson Mafwele, following allegations of serious human rights violatio...

item.title: APC Presidential Primaries in Katsina Record High Turnout as Radda Expresses Confidence in Tinubu’s 2027 Re-election
item.description: Katsina State Governor, Dikko Radda, don express strong confidence say President Bola Ahmed Tinubu and all All Progressives Congress (APC) candidates go win big for the 2027 general elections. Dis sta...

item.title: Kenya’s fuel prices remain high but stable amid regional shortages and rationing in East Africa
item.description: Across East Africa, the fuel situation shows major differences in both pricing and availability, with Kenya sitting in a middle position compared to its neighbours. The article highlights that while K...

item.title: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif begins four-day official visit to China, arrives in Hangzhou
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has arrived in Hangzhou, China, marking the start of his four-day official visit aimed at strengthening Pakistan-China relations and expanding economic cooperation. Upon ...

item.title: REA Signs Partnerships With NCX and YIN to Expand Renewable Energy, Agriculture, and Innovation Development in Nigeria
item.description: The Rural Electrification Agency (REA) don sign two major Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) with the Nigerian Commodity Exchange (NCX) and the Young Innovators of Nigeria (YIN) to support Nigeria’s cl...

item.title: ADC confirms Halliru Jika as governorship candidate for Bauchi 2027 election
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has officially confirmed former Senator Halliru Jika as its governorship candidate for the 2027 Bauchi State election. The confirmation was carried out during an ...

item.title: Central Mindanao leaders praise WestMinCom chief Donald Gumiran for peacebuilding efforts
item.description: Local government officials in Central Mindanao expressed support and appreciation for the special recognition given to Western Mindanao Command chief Lt. Gen. Donald Gumiran by TopNewsAsia Magazine. G...

item.title: Te Maire Martin cited for late contact on Kyle Flanagan in NRL clash
item.description: Te Maire Martin of the New Zealand Warriors has been placed on report early in an NRL match following an incident involving St George Illawarra Dragons half Kyle Flanagan. The alleged offence relates ...

item.title: African parliamentarians visit UCT to review advances in tuberculosis vaccine and diagnostic research
item.description: A delegation of African parliamentarians and global health representatives recently visited the University of Cape Town (UCT), where they were briefed on significant progress in tuberculosis (TB) rese...
